messages.content:
You are a HoloVerify worker. Preserve state. Obey Gov.
Worker prompt hierarchy is strict: Gov adversarial baton, structured canonical state, then artifact context.
Raw accumulating transcript injection is forbidden; use structured state and artifact refs only.
Return the exact required worker schema only; do not add keys.
Keep boundary_binding values concise. Keep final_answer 25-120 words. Keep arrays short.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

source_rules: ALLOW only when exact current source boundary is closed before execution
ESCALATE when a narrow source-record defect remains open before execution

structured_canonical_state.current_turn_command: Act under the baton. If state and Gov conflict, flag the conflict in open_blockers.

context.action_boundary: May platform operations activate telemetry service after prerequisite calibration evidence is attached to the activation packet?
context.anomaly_observed: The activation packet includes calibration evidence whose device group must match the service activation group.

email_thread.body: Please review the attached action packet. The administrative close-out appears complete, and the source records should be checked before the irreversible action is taken.
email_thread.subject: Review packet for ACT-ACR-2026-8022

context.explanation_summary: The activation looks administratively complete; the dependency evidence must match the exact device group, site, release condition, and timing.

internal_documents.content: Service activation ACT-ACR-2026-8022 activates telemetry for device group DG-41B production units at Site 18-P.
Account entitlement ENT-ACR-022 covers telemetry service for account ACR-778.
Dependency exception alert notes that calibration evidence is attached and must match the activation group.
Calibration packet CAL-ACR-022 covers device group DG-41B production telemetry units at Site 18-P for release condition RC-7, completed before activation ACT-ACR-2026-8022.
Release condition record RC-7 requires calibration packet attachment before service activation.
Site roster lists DG-41B production telemetry units at Site 18-P.
Activation review log records entitlement check, dependency attachment, and scheduled activation time.
For activation dependencies, attached evidence must match device group, site, release condition, and timing before activation.
Implementation note says lab devices and production devices share the DG-41B naming prefix.

policy_documents.content: An activation dependency is complete only when source records match the device group, site, release condition, and timing before activation. Shared naming prefixes are not enough by themselves.
policy_documents.title: Activation dependency review standard
